summary: Verified ES 'VTC' → 'Minivan'/'Minibús' rebrand + new /es/minivan-7-plazas and /es/minibus-8-plazas URLs. All 6 test scenarios pass: (1) new ES URLs render Spanish content with no 'VTC' in body, (2) H1s start with 'Minivan 7 Plazas' and 'Minibús 8 Plazas', (3) booking form titles 'Reserva tu minivan/minibús ... plazas' present, (4) canonical + hreflang='es' point to the new slugs, (5) backward-compat /es/vtc-7-plazas and /es/vtc-8-plazas still resolve and render the same ES page (canonical rewrites to new URL), (6) FR/EN regression preserved — /vtc-7-places still shows 'Taxi / VTC 7 Places' h1 and /vtc-7-seats shows '7-Seat Minivan Transfer' h1. Sitemap.xml: new canonical <loc> entries present, old /es/vtc-*-plazas <loc> removed, hreflang='es' updated.
critical_code_review_comments: CityTransferPage auto-detect language useEffect (lines 60-73) only switches language to ES when currentPath exactly matches seoUrls[es]. When users land directly on the backward-compat URL /es/vtc-7-plazas or /es/vtc-8-plazas, no seoUrl matches, so language remains whatever localStorage has. In this test the previous language was 'es' so ES rendered correctly, but a fresh user (default FR) landing on /es/vtc-7-plazas would see the page in the wrong language. Consider adding legacy alias entries or a fallback path prefix check ('/es/' → force 'es') to guarantee ES rendering on backward-compat URLs.
Sitemap keeps hreflang='x-default' pointing to /vtc-7-places and /vtc-8-places (FR). If ES is intended as the preferred canonical for Spanish speakers, that is fine — just documenting.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified content rename (VTC→Minivan/Minibús in ES) on /es/minivan-7-plazas, /es/minibus-8-plazas, /es/vtc-7-plazas alias, /es/vtc-8-plazas alias, plus FR/EN regression on /vtc-7-places & /vtc-7-seats, plus sitemap.xml. Language auto-detect on backward-compat aliases relies on previous localStorage state — noted in code review comments but not a blocker per current requirements.
